dataset.description: The Telco customer churn dataset contains information about a fictional telco company that provided home phone and Internet services to 7043 customers in California in Q3. It indicates which customers have left, stayed, or signed up for their service. Multiple important demographics are included for each customer, as well as a Satisfaction Score, Churn Score, and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) index. It is designed to facilitate analysis of customer behavior and retention strategies.

desc: The Telco customer churn dataset contains information about a fictional telco company that provided home phone and Internet services to 7043 customers in California in Q3. It indicates which customers have left, stayed, or signed up for their service. Multiple important demographics are included for each customer, as well as a Satisfaction Score, Churn Score, and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) index. It is designed to facilitate analysis of customer behavior and retention strategies.
goal: Analyze customer groups based on their status (churned, retained, newly signed up) by comparing key metrics (satisfaction, churn scores, CLTV) and demographic characteristics, to identify potential factors influencing customer churn and retention.
name: Visual Analytics System telco_goal3_1742640209

effect.description: Filters customer_status to a specific group (e.g., Stayed, Churned, Joined) and updates subsequent views.

visualization.description: Displays the mean satisfaction score, churn score, and CLTV for each customer status group (Stayed, Churned, Joined) using group-specific bar charts.
encodings: y: aggregated means of Status_Analysis.satisfaction_score, Status_Analysis.churn_score, and Status_Analysis.cltv

effect.description: Selects specific ranges of values on the box plots to refine analysis focus.

visualization.description: Displays box plots for satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV segmented by customer_status to show the spread, central tendency, and outliers for each group.

effect.description: Displays customer details in tooltips for specific heat map cells.

visualization.description: Heat map displaying correlations between service usage metrics (e.g., internet_service, phone_service) and customer_status groups.
encodings: x: Service_Options.internet_service, Service_Options.phone_service, Service_Options.multiple_lines, Online_Services.online_security, Online_Services.premium_tech_support
color: correlation coefficient between service usage and customer_status

effect.description: Highlights or compares distributions for selected demographic features.

visualization.description: Stacked bar charts showing proportions of gender, senior_citizen, dependents, and married status across customer_status groups.

views.components: Add a table component for showing individual customer profiles with sortable columns.

effect.description: Updates and displays detailed information for selected customer profiles.

visualization.description: Detailed panel or table showing all associated data for selected customer profiles, including metrics, demographics, and service usage.
encodings: rows: Detail fields linked to individual customers

data.reason: These metrics are critical for understanding group-level differences in customer behavior and value.
data.requirement: High-level summaries of customer groups (Stayed, Churned, Joined) for satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV.

data.reason: Demographics provide additional context that can help understand the underlying drivers of customer decisions.
data.requirement: Demographic details for each customer status group to identify contextual factors influencing churn or retention.

data.reason: Satisfaction is a key determinant of customer churn and retention.
data.requirement: Satisfaction score distribution across customer groups to identify group-level differences in customer satisfaction.

data.reason: CLTV is a vital metric for evaluating customer lifetime value and business metrics.
data.requirement: Distribution of CLTV across customer groups to assess the financial impact of different customer statuses.

data.reason: Understanding service usage patterns can help identify customer behaviors that predict churn.
data.requirement: Service-related data across customer groups to determine the impact of specific services on churn or retention.

data.reason: Detailed views are necessary for actionable insights and understanding individual cases.
data.requirement: Drill-down capabilities to individual customer profiles for detailed analysis of specific customers.

data.reason: Revenue-related metrics are crucial for evaluating the economic impact of customer churn.
data.requirement: Financial data across customer groups to assess the financial contributions and patterns related to churn.

interaction.reason: Filtering is essential for isolating and analyzing individual groups of interest.
interaction.requirement: Customer_status filtering to allow users to focus on specific customer groups.

interaction.reason: Brushing and selection help users identify and explore specific statistically significant ranges.
interaction.requirement: Brushing/selection to highlight specific ranges on box plots.

interaction.reason: Tooltips are valuable for conveying detailed information without cluttering the visualization.
interaction.requirement: Hover tooltips to provide additional insights for each data point in secondary visualizations.

interaction.reason: Drill-downs provide the granular insights needed for actionable analysis.
interaction.requirement: Clickable drill-downs to allow detailed inspection of individual customer profiles.

interaction.reason: Interactive selection allows users to focus on the most relevant data points.
interaction.requirement: Selection of demographic features to dynamically highlight or compare distributions.

interaction.reason: Zooming and panning enhance the detail and readability of trend analyses.
interaction.requirement: Zooming/panning for line charts or area charts to analyze trends over different time periods or group sizes.

visualization.reason: Bar charts are suitable for displaying comparisons among categorical data.
visualization.requirement: Bar charts to compare mean satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV for different customer groups.

visualization.reason: Box plots are ideal for visualizing the distribution, range, and central tendencies of continuous data.
visualization.requirement: Box plots to observe the spread and central tendency of satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV for different customer groups.

visualization.reason: Heat maps are useful for showing complex relationships between multiple variables.
visualization.requirement: Heat maps to visualize correlations between service usage and customer_status.

visualization.reason: Stacked bar charts are appropriate for visualizing the distribution of categorical variables within groups.
visualization.requirement: Stacked bar charts to show proportions of demographic features within each customer status group.

visualization.reason: Scatter plots are useful for visualizing the relationship between two continuous variables.
visualization.requirement: Scatter plots to investigate correlations between churn_score and CLTV, color-coded by customer_status.

visualization.reason: Line charts or area charts are suitable for visualizing trends over time.
visualization.requirement: Line charts or area charts to trend satisfaction_score or churn_score over tenure for different customer groups.

visualization.reason: Custom visualizations allow for complex data representations and clustering.
visualization.requirement: Custom bubble charts or stacked bar charts to cluster churn reasons and promotional offers.

tasks.method: Use bar charts (V1) to compare the mean satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV for Stayed, Churned, and Joined groups. Implement customer_status filtering (I1).
tasks.objective: Identify group-level differences in satisfaction, churn risk, and customer value to assess underlying patterns.
tasks.type: characterizing distributions | retrieving values | computing derived values

tasks.method: Create box plots (V2) segmented by customer_status to observe the spread, central tendency, and outliers for satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV. Enable brushing/selection (I2) to highlight specific ranges on the box plots.
tasks.objective: Understand the distribution spread for key metrics and identify outliers or unusual patterns in each group.

tasks.method: Use a heat map (V3) to visualize correlations between service usage (e.g., online_security, multiple_lines) and customer_status. Enable hover tooltips (I3) for customer details in each heat map cell.
tasks.objective: Explore specific service usage patterns clustered with customer_status to identify behavioral factors influencing churn or retention.

tasks.method: Use stacked bar charts (V4) to show proportions of gender, senior_citizen, dependents, and married status within each customer_status group. Allow selection of demographic features (I5) to dynamically highlight or compare distributions.
tasks.objective: Provide demographic insights into different customer groups, revealing contextual factors influencing churn or retention.

tasks.method: Create a scatter plot (V5) with churn_score vs. CLTV, color-coded by customer_status. Use satisfaction_score as point size. Enable zooming/panning (I6) and tooltips (I3).
tasks.objective: Investigate correlations and trends among churn likelihood, customer value, and satisfaction scores for different groups.

tasks.method: Use line charts or area charts (V6) to trend satisfaction_score or churn_score over tenure for Stayed, Churned, and Joined groups. Add filtering by customer_status (I1) and panning/zooming (I6).
tasks.objective: Understand temporal changes in churn risk and customer satisfaction across differing customer lifecycles.
tasks.type: retrieving values | determining ranges | characterizing distributions

tasks.method: Use a heat map (V3) to find correlations between entertainment service types (e.g., streaming_tv, internet_type) and customer_status. Enable hover tooltips (I3) and brushing (I2) for interactivity.
tasks.objective: Identify entertainment-related services influencing customer churn or retention behaviors.

tasks.method: Enable clickable drill-downs (I4) from group summaries to individual customer profiles, showing all associated data through a detailed table or panel.
tasks.objective: Allow detailed inspection of individual customers and their demographics, service usage, and status metrics, enabling actionable insights.

tasks.method: Use bar charts (V1) or box plots (V2) to compare the revenue-related variables segmented by customer_status. Add customer_status filtering (I1).
tasks.objective: Examine financial contributions of different groups, identifying anomalies or revenue patterns associated with churned customers.

tasks.method: Cluster churn reasons and promotional offers using bubble charts or stacked bar charts (custom visualization). Use drill-down (I4) for more details.
tasks.objective: Determine whether specific offers or payment methods correlate with common churn reasons, providing strategic promotional insights.

V1.description: Bar charts to compare mean satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV for different customer groups.

V2.description: Box plots to observe the spread and central tendency of satisfaction_score, churn_score, and CLTV for different customer groups.

V3.description: Heat maps to visualize correlations between service usage and customer_status.

V4.description: Stacked bar charts to show proportions of demographic features within each customer status group.

V5.description: Scatter plots to investigate correlations between churn_score and CLTV, color-coded by customer_status.

V6.description: Line charts or area charts to trend satisfaction_score or churn_score over tenure for different customer groups.

V7.description: Custom bubble charts or stacked bar charts to cluster churn reasons and promotional offers.
